report_data:
2.1.1 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Concrete/Asphalt - Damages Present
Cracks and/or settling materials present at time of inspection. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.2 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Sidewalk/Driveway Expansion Joints
Replace organic materials (wood) in sidewalk/driveway expansion joints with proper elements resistant materials. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.1 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Flashings - Maintain Caulking
Maintain caulking on all exposed nail heads and/or joints to prevent water entry. Caulking is not an ideal form of waterproofing for more than an occasional plumbing boot nail head and is a temporary fix.  Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.2 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Roofing - Improper Clearance
Roofing/wall connections should be installed with a 2in clearance to help keep wall materials from damage. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.3 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Vent(s) - Damaged
Repair/replace damaged and/or rusty roofing vents. Make all repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.1 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Insulation - Pest Damage
Insulation appears to have been damaged by pests. Trails present where rodents traffic. Close up all attic accesses at roofing/soffit connections. Review the link below to learn more. 

https://youtube.com/shorts/DiaGqJlaJ2s?si=Hi52gl0kzCAaO-AV

Roughly 80% of homes we inspect have this condition of rodent accesses and presence of rodents in the attic. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified insulation contractor.

2.4.2 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Rodents - Accesses Present
Voids present in dwelling that allow for easy access into attic for rodents. Close up all attic accesses and include all connections at soffit/roofing to prevent rodent entry.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.3 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Decking - Sagging (Pre-Owned Home)
Areas of roof present that are showing signs of sagging or raised decking. This is a common occurrence at most homes.  Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified roofing professional.

2.4.4 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Insulation - Attic Ladder Baffles Missing
Install baffle around attic ladder to keep insulation in attic.. Remove any damaged insulation, investigate all areas, and install additional insulation to reach R-38 value.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified insulation contractor.

2.4.5 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Insulation - Insufficient Depth/Missing
Insulation depth may be inadequate in some areas of attic and/or walls. Remove any damaged insulation, investigate all areas, and install additional insulation to reach R-38 value.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified insulation contractor.

2.5.1 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Interior Cracks - Minor
Cracking was observed in drywall with previous repairs. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified drywall contractor.

2.5.2 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Interior - Wallboard Damaged/Previous Repairs
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.3 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Grout - Damaged
Grout cracking and/or damaged, likely due to improper materials or setting time. Cracks in wall near wet areas (showers, tubs) can provide a way for water to enter wall cavity. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.4 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Exterior Cracks - Minor
Minor cracking was observed in exterior wall(s). Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Recommended DIY Project

2.5.5 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Exterior Mortar Cracking
Cracking at mortar joints present. Mortar has very little sheer strength and cracks relatively easily when stress is introduced. Large mortar joints are also more susceptible to cracking. Recommend using sanded silicone grout after removing all loose materials. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Recommended DIY Project

2.5.6 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Wallboard Damages/Voids
Cracks and/or damage present at exterior wallboard and/or gaps present between materials. Caulking and in some cases with large gaps, flashings can be used to make repairs. Seek further investigation of all walls and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.7 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Facia/Trim Damages Present
Caulk and secure all damaged wood trim and materials. Make replacements as necessary. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.8 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Wallboard Wavy - Exterior
Wavy wallboard present likely due to slightly post flush installed studs and/or out of square sill plates. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.9 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Lintels - Rusted
Lintels without paint rust much faster than painted lintels. Wire brush and paint all rusted lintels and/or lintels missing paint. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.10 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Expansion Joints - Damaged
Remove damaged materials or caulking present in joints and replace. Expansion joints or control joints help to direct cracking to a specific area of the wall. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.6.1 I. Structural Systems: F. Ceilings and Floors - Ceiling - Minor Damage
Minor damage or deterioration to the ceiling was visible at the time of the inspection. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified drywall contractor.

2.6.2 I. Structural Systems: F. Ceilings and Floors - Flooring - Damaged
The home flooring had general moderate damage visible at the time of the inspection. Did not evaluate flooring for cosmetic damages only physical damages that may effect performance. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.6.3 I. Structural Systems: F. Ceilings and Floors - Flooring - Not Level
Section(s) of flooring not level. Areas present where beam(s)/concrete/tile can be felt raised or lowered under foot. With these symptoms, it seems that little floating preparation was done to concrete before flooring was installed. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.6.4 I. Structural Systems: F. Ceilings and Floors - Flooring - Decking Squeaking
Decking under flooring squeaking. This is a common occurrence for this age of home. Monitor and make repairs as necessary
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.6.5 I. Structural Systems: F. Ceilings and Floors - Flooring - Tiles Cracked/Loose
one or more tiles showing cracks or deflection when tapped. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.1 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Door - Out of Square
Door out of square. Seek further investigation of all doors and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.2 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Bi-Fold Doors Out of Square
Doors out of square or gaps inconsistent between doors. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.3 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Door(s) Damaged
One or more doors observed to be damaged and/or missing. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.4 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Gaps at Door Casings
Distance between door casings and door too wide. Light can be seen from next room due to crack in opening. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.5 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Stain/Seal Coat Exterior Door(s)
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.8.1 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Damaged Window(s)
One or more windows appears to have general damage, but are operational. Recommend a window professional seek further investigation of all windows/flashings and clean, lubricate and adjust all windows. Make all repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified window repair/installation contractor.

2.8.2 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Windows Difficult To Open/Close
One or more windows were difficult to open/close and may have spring damages. Make all repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.8.3 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Window Spring(s) Damaged
One or more windows have damaged springs/strings which make windows difficult to open. Consider window professional to evaluate all windows and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified window repair/installation contractor.

2.8.4 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Window - Screens Damaged/Missing
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified window repair/installation contractor.

2.8.5 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Water Damage Near Window Casing and Sill
Water damage likely from missing flashings over windows or perhaps from condensate. Sills and/or casing near bottom of windows damaged. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.8.6 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Blinds - Damaged
Seek further investigation and make necessary repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.9.1 I. Structural Systems: I. Stairways (Interior and Exterior) - Baluster Gap Too Wide
The baluster space is not up to modern safety standards. The space between balusters should not allow passage of a 4 3/8-inch sphere for child safety. The railing here may also not be high enough. Recommend a qualified handyman or original installer repair and bring up to code. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified handyman.

2.9.2 I. Structural Systems: I. Stairways (Interior and Exterior) - Loose Rail/Balusters
Handrail/balusters were loose. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified handyman.

2.10.1 I. Structural Systems: J. Fireplaces and Chimneys - Water Damages and Rust Present
Rust present at chimney components likely due to a leaking/missing chimney cap, spark arrestor, flashings or other void. Standing water at chimney caps can cause water entry and rust on interior components. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.10.2 I. Structural Systems: J. Fireplaces and Chimneys - Chimney Flue/Brick/Liner Dirty
Chimney liner had layer of creosote dust, so underlying structure couldn't be inspected for cracks. Recommend qualified chimney sweep company inspect and/or clean.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified chimney contractor.

2.11.1 I. Structural Systems: K. Porches, Balconies, Decks, and Carports - Deck - Wood/Materials Damaged
One or more decking, boards or railings were observed to be loose or damaged. Make repairs as necessary 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified deck contractor.

2.11.2 I. Structural Systems: K. Porches, Balconies, Decks, and Carports - Damaged Ceiling
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.12.1 I. Structural Systems: L. Other - Cabinet Doors Out Of Square/Damaged
Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.12.2 I. Structural Systems: L. Other - Countertop - Damaged
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.12.3 I. Structural Systems: L. Other - Mirror - Damaged
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.1.1 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Wiring - Red/Black Wires Present
Breakers with red wires must be connected to their black wire counterparts. This outdated wiring method can lead to electrocution because the circuit may not be completely turned off. Conduct a thorough investigation and make all necessary repairs.
The scenario described indicates a potentially dangerous, out-of-code, or improper installation of a Multi-Wire Branch Circuit (MWBC). In a safe, proper installation, red and black wires (representing two different hot phases) must be tied together at the breaker so they disconnect simultaneously. 
If these wires are on separate single-pole breakers, it is a significant shock hazard because shutting off one breaker does not guarantee the circuit is completely de-energized, leading to potential electrocution, especially when working on split-receptacles or shared neutral devices. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.1.2 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Main Panel Not Bonded
The main panel does mot have a visible bond/ground electrode. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.2.1 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Receptacles: GFCI Not Present/Damaged
Wet areas and exterior receptacles are required to have GFCI protected outlets. In some areas this protection at receptacles is damaged or missing, seek further investigation of all receptacles and make all repairs.
GFCI (Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter) is an automatic device that offers personal protection against lethal electrical shock or electrocution. It is a special electrical receptacle or outlet that can stop electrical power within milliseconds as a safety precaution.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.2 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Receptacles: No Exterior GFCI Protection
Exterior receptacles not energized, receptacle damaged. Some exterior receptacles not GFCI protected. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.3 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Fixture: Fan Wobbly/Damaged
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.2.4 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Fixtures: Smoke / CO Detectors Missing / Damaged
Modern standards require Smoke detectors in all bedrooms and combination Smoke/CO detectors in hallways adjacent to bedrooms. Further the detectors need to be interconnected electrically so that when one detector alarms, all the others alarm also. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.2.5 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Receptacles: Covers Damaged/Missing
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.6 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Switches: Purpose Not Determined
What does this switch operate? One or more switches did not operate. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.7 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Fixtures: Light(s) Inoperable
One or more lighting fixtures were inoperable. This may be due to a bad bulb or some other unknown condition. This condition should be further evaluated and corrected as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.8 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Fixture: Missing/Damaged Globe
Make repairs as necessary.

3.2.9 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Fixtures: Loose/ Not Flush
Fixture was observed to be loose / not flush with drywall.  Make repairs as necessary. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

4.1.1 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: A. Heating Equipment - Gas Supply - No Drip Leg
Recommend installing drip leg on gas supply to catch condensates and debris. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified heating and cooling contractor

4.2.1 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Under-performing HVAC
Evaporator and condenser coil data tags. Seek licensed technician to diagnose entire HVAC systems, perform leak detection tests, provide written details of tests performed and operational temperatures and pressures of both heating and cooling systems and make all repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified HVAC professional.

4.2.2 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - HVAC Damaged Component
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

4.2.3 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - HVAC Cabinet Damaged
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

4.2.4 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Operational Service Life Reached
Systems may have reached their useful/intended life span. Seek further investigation by qualified HVAC technician, make all repairs and quote replacements. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified heating and cooling contractor

4.2.5 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Water/Debris in Secondary Pan
Remove debris from secondary pan under evaporator coil in attic. Debris may cause premature clogging of condensate line. Clean and service condensate line. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified HVAC professional.

4.2.6 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Condensate Line/Insulation Missing or Damaged
Missing or damaged insulation on refrigerant line can cause energy loss and condensation which may damage finish materials and insulation. Replace insulation on entire HVAC low pressure condensate line. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified HVAC professional.

4.2.7 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Condensate Drain - Improper Location
Condensate drain line ran to improper location. Recommend qualified technician to run proper drain. Seek further investigation and make necessary repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

4.3.1 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: C. Duct Systems, Chases, and Vents - Evaluate/Balance HVAC Zones
Engage HVAC technician to evaluate zones to balance heating/cooling for a more even dispersal throughout home. Consider installation of additional zones and thermostat(s) to balance areas which are experiencing variances in temperature. A solution may be to group zones by Master Bedroom, Common Areas and additional Bedrooms to achieve a greater level of comfort and temperature control. Seek further investigation by HVAC technician and make all repairs. HVAC technician to diagnose condensate lines and entire HVAC system while on property to determine if any deficiencies present and make all repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

4.3.2 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: C. Duct Systems, Chases, and Vents - Explore/Clean Ductwork
Dirty substances present on plenum/ductwork/registers. This is a common occurrence in many homes, evaluation and cleaning by an HVAC technician is advised.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified HVAC professional.

4.3.3 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: C. Duct Systems, Chases, and Vents - Vent Damaged
Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Fixture Covered by Soil/Water
Seek removal of water/soil so that fixtures may be operated. Unable to determine what type of fixture is installed in box or if a fixture is present. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.2 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Damaged/Missing Fixtures
Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.3 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Housings/Covers Damaged/Missing
Install housing covers and make flush with wall/grade. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.4 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Improperly Installed/Damaged Sink
Improper installation and/or supports at sinks. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.5 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Fixture - Toilet Installed Improperly
Toilet(s) are installed improperly (loose). Water leaks are frequently caused by loose toilets. Seek further investigation to determine if leaks and water damage are present and make all repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified plumbing contractor.

5.1.6 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Fixture - Loose in Wall
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified plumbing contractor.

5.1.7 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Insulate Plumbing Supply
All supply in close proximity and near exterior walls should be insulated or distanced from each other properly. Seek further investigation of all water supplies and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.8 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Insulate Exterior Plumbing Supply
Exposed water supply lines may freeze in cold weather. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.2.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: B. Drains, Wastes, and Vents - Diverter(s)/Drain Stopper Damaged
Drain diverter damaged and/or difficult to operate. Seek further investigation of all diverters/drain stoppers and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.2.2 IV. Plumbing Systems: B. Drains, Wastes, and Vents - Install Backwater Valve
A backwater valve prohibits water from the street and other homes in the neighborhood from entering the drain system under your house. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.2.3 IV. Plumbing Systems: B. Drains, Wastes, and Vents - Main Drain - Standing Water/Debris
The drain is dirty and standing water/debris is present. The camera had a difficult time visualizing the drain due to debris.

Blockages, dirty drain walls, and water present which may render the sewer camera ineffective and unable to uncover deficiencies. Seek plumber to clean out all drains, diagnose by running sewer camera both to house and to main sewer, consider hydrostatic testing, and back flow valve installation, along with video documentation of the line after repairs/cleaning and make all repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.2.4 IV. Plumbing Systems: B. Drains, Wastes, and Vents - Main Drain - Belly In Pipe
There appears to be a belly in the drain line.  A belly in the pipe means there is a low spot holding water. Bellies can accumulate debris and cause clogging of the pipes due to poor drainage. Unable to inspect drain fully. Clean out drain line and perform, another evaluation, and make all repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.3.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: C. Water Heating Equipment - Gas Supply - No Drip Leg
Water heater(s) are not equipped with a drip leg or sediment trap on the gas line as per modern standards. Recommend corrections be performed by a qualified plumbing contractor.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.3.2 IV. Plumbing Systems: C. Water Heating Equipment - Water Heater Leak
Water was observed at bottom of water heater. Seek further investigation and make necessary repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.3.3 IV. Plumbing Systems: C. Water Heating Equipment - Lifespan Reached
Seek replacement water heater in the near term.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.5.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: F. Gas Distribution Systems and Gas Appliances - Gas Line Not Bonded
Gas line did not appear to be bonded as per current requirements.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.5.2 IV. Plumbing Systems: F. Gas Distribution Systems and Gas Appliances - Rusted Gas Supply
Please be advised all gas piping should be painted in order to prevent rust and corrosion. High quality rust oleum paint is usually the best for metal, steel or galvanized piping. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.1.1 V. Appliances: A. Dishwashers - Improper Installation
Installed crooked/loose under countertop. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.3.1 V. Appliances: C. Range Hood and Exhaust Systems - Exhaust System Missing
No exhaust system present to prevent moisture and grease in kitchen area. Recommend qualified contractor install range hood or exhaust system.
Here is a resource on choosing a range hood. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.4.1 V. Appliances: D. Ranges, Cooktops, and Ovens - Range Not Fastened
Range was not fastened securely. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified handyman.

6.4.2 V. Appliances: D. Ranges, Cooktops, and Ovens - Improper Installation
Range not installed fully against wall. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.6.1 V. Appliances: F. Mechanical Exhaust Vents and Bathroom Heaters - Mechanical Vent(s) Not Present
Install proper mechanical exhaust vent(s) and/or extensions ducted through exterior wall to remove moist air from living and attic spaces. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.7.1 V. Appliances: G. Garage Door Operators - Auto Reverse Sensor(s) Not Working
The auto reverse sensor was not responding at time of inspection. Close force may need to be adjusted so door retreats when encountering an obstacle. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified garage door contractor.

7.1.1 VI. Optional Systems: A. Landscape Irrigation (Sprinkler) Systems - Adjust Sprinkler/Improve Head Placement
Sprinkler head spraying side of house, concrete flatwork, and/or fencing, improve placement. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.1 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Concrete/Asphalt - Damages Present
Cracks and/or settling materials present at time of inspection. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.2 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Sidewalk/Driveway Expansion Joints
Replace organic materials (wood) in sidewalk/driveway expansion joints with proper elements resistant materials. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.1 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Flashings - Maintain Caulking
Maintain caulking on all exposed nail heads and/or joints to prevent water entry. Caulking is not an ideal form of waterproofing for more than an occasional plumbing boot nail head and is a temporary fix.  Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.2 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Roofing - Damaged Coverings
Roof coverings exhibited general damage that is common for this age of home. Symptoms present that may indicate repairs, impact damages and/or asphalt fleck loss and damaged shingles. Professional roofer to inspect all areas for damage, remove all debris and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified roofing professional.

2.3.3 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Roofing - Improper Clearance
Roofing/wall connections should be installed with a 2in clearance to help keep wall materials from damage. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.1 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Rodents - Accesses Present
Voids present in dwelling that allow for easy access into attic for rodents. Close up all attic accesses and include all connections at soffit/roofing to prevent rodent entry.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.2 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Decking - Sagging
Areas of roof present that are sagging or raised. This is a common occurrence at most homes. At times a builder may provide a letter certifying that the roof decking is within form and level. A sample document has been provided below. Monitor and make repairs to all effected areas as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified roofing professional.

2.4.3 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Ventilation - Insufficient
Dispersal of ventilation could be improved to decrease heat in attic. Add ventilation near ridge(s) and/or baffles near soffits to allow for passive flow of cool air from soffits to ridge vents. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.4 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Truss - Previous Repairs
Repairs to engineered truss required engineers letter. Builder to provide letter certifying repairs from engineers. Seek further investigation of all truss and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.5 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Truss - Remove Unnecessary Blocking
Remove unnecessary materials between trusses. In some cases this material may contribute to irregularities forming in roof decking. Seek further investigation of all blocking and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.1 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Interior - Wallboard Damaged/Previous Repairs
Make repairs as necessary.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.1.1 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Wiring - Red/Black Wires Present
Breakers with red wires must be connected to their black wire counterparts. This outdated wiring method can lead to electrocution because the circuit may not be completely turned off. Conduct a thorough investigation and make all necessary repairs.
The scenario described indicates a potentially dangerous, out-of-code, or improper installation of a Multi-Wire Branch Circuit (MWBC). In a safe, proper installation, red and black wires (representing two different hot phases) must be tied together at the breaker so they disconnect simultaneously. 
If these wires are on separate single-pole breakers, it is a significant shock hazard because shutting off one breaker does not guarantee the circuit is completely de-energized, leading to potential electrocution, especially when working on split-receptacles or shared neutral devices. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.3.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: C. Water Heating Equipment - Rust In Water
Rust in water likely from rust buildup in water heater tank or corrosion of plumbing supplies. Seek plumber to asses water heater and replace/make repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.3.2 IV. Plumbing Systems: C. Water Heating Equipment - Relief Valve Damaged
Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.3.3 IV. Plumbing Systems: C. Water Heating Equipment - Egg Smelling Water Present
May be due to buildup of solids in water heater. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.1.1 V. Appliances: A. Dishwashers - Improper Installation

Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.3.1 V. Appliances: C. Range Hood and Exhaust Systems - Range Hood/Vent Damaged
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.3.2 V. Appliances: C. Range Hood and Exhaust Systems - Exhaust to Interior - Not Fully Connected

Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.4.1 V. Appliances: D. Ranges, Cooktops, and Ovens - Oven Underperforming
Oven temp (set at 350 F.) must perform within 25 degrees of set temp, seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.6.1 V. Appliances: F. Mechanical Exhaust Vents and Bathroom Heaters - Damaged/Noisy Exhaust Vent
Repair/replace noisy damaged vent and or motor. Make repairs as necessary.

6.6.2 V. Appliances: F. Mechanical Exhaust Vents and Bathroom Heaters - Repair Fan/Vent Cover(s)
Make repairs as necessary.

Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.1 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Concrete/Asphalt - Damages Present
Cracks and/or settling materials present at time of inspection. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.2 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Slab - Wavy
Slab is wavy in one or more areas through perimeter. Crooked forms during foundation pour phase of construction may cause wavy looking perimeter wall. Seek further investigation and make repairs as necessary. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.1 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Flashings - Maintain Caulking
Maintain caulking on all exposed nail heads and/or joints to prevent water entry. Caulking is not an ideal form of waterproofing for more than an occasional plumbing boot nail head and is a temporary fix.  Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.2 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Roofing - Damaged Coverings
Roof coverings exhibited general damage that is common for this age of home. Symptoms present that may indicate repairs, impact damages and/or asphalt fleck loss and damaged shingles. Professional roofer to inspect all areas for damage, remove all debris and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified roofing professional.

2.3.3 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Flashings - Advisement
Flashings at the bottom of a steep roof like this dead-ending into a wall is a likely spot for a water leak. Keep an eye on this location and ensure proper maintenance and appropriate flashings present. Seek further investigation of all flashings and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.3.4 I. Structural Systems: C. Roof Covering Materials - Flashings - Not Present Over Walkway(s)
Install proper flashings to direct water around walkways and/or HVAC condensers. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.1 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Rodents - Accesses Present
Voids present in dwelling that allow for easy access into attic for rodents. Close up all attic accesses and include all connections at soffit/roofing to prevent rodent entry.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.4.2 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Decking - Sagging
Areas of roof present that are sagging or raised. This is a common occurrence at most homes. At times a builder may provide a letter certifying that the roof decking is within form and level. A sample document has been provided below. Monitor and make repairs to all effected areas as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified roofing professional.

2.4.3 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Insulation - Insufficient Depth/Missing
Insulation depth may be inadequate in some areas of attic and/or walls. Remove any damaged insulation, investigate all areas, and install additional insulation to reach R-38 value.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified insulation contractor.

2.4.4 I. Structural Systems: D. Roof Structures and Attics - Attic Pulldown Ladder Damaged
Attic pulldown ladder at the garage was damaged, bent and/or not working properly.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.1 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Interior - Wallboard Damaged/Previous Repairs
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.2 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Wallboard Wavy - Interior
Wavy wallboard present in one or more areas likely due to slightly post flush installed studs and/or out of square sill plates. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.3 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Exterior Mortar Cracking
Cracking at mortar joints present. Mortar has very little sheer strength and cracks relatively easily when stress is introduced. Large mortar joints are also more susceptible to cracking. Recommend using sanded silicone grout after removing all loose materials. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Recommended DIY Project

2.5.4 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Wallboard Damages/Voids
Cracks and/or damage present at exterior wallboard and/or gaps present between materials. Caulking and in some cases with large gaps, flashings can be used to make repairs. Seek further investigation of all walls and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.5.5 I. Structural Systems: E. Walls (Interior and Exterior) - Vent(s) - Missing Garage Wall Vents
Install garage vents in walls to vent garage space properly from toxic fumes. Seek further investigation of all ventilation and make all repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.6.1 I. Structural Systems: F. Ceilings and Floors - Ceiling - Previous Repairs
Drywall/paneling at ceiling and walls damaged and previous repairs may be present. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.1 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Door - Out of Square
Door out of square. Seek further investigation of all doors and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.2 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Bi-Fold Doors Out of Square
Doors out of square or gaps inconsistent between doors. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.7.3 I. Structural Systems: G. Doors (Interior and Exterior) - Door Not Flush With Trim
Door(s) warped and not flush when closed. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.8.1 I. Structural Systems: H. Windows - Window Spring(s) Damaged
One or more windows have damaged springs/strings which make windows difficult to open. Consider window professional to evaluate all windows and make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified window repair/installation contractor.

2.9.1 I. Structural Systems: I. Stairways (Interior and Exterior) - Handrail Missing/Damaged
Handrail must be gripable and extend to bottom tread. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified handyman.

2.12.1 I. Structural Systems: L. Other - Damaged Fencing
Replace or repair all damaged and sagging posts, fencing pickets, and gates. Make all repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified fencing contractor

2.12.2 I. Structural Systems: L. Other - Cabinets/Drawers- Difficult to Open/Close
Seek further investigation of all cabinet drawers and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.12.3 I. Structural Systems: L. Other - Cabinets Missing Components
Shelving, knobs or hinges missing or damaged. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.1.1 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Main Panel Not Bonded
The main panel does mot have a visible bond/ground electrode. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

3.1.2 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Panel - Damaged
Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.1 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Receptacles: GFCI Not Present/Damaged
Wet areas and exterior receptacles are required to have GFCI protected outlets. In some areas this protection at receptacles is damaged or missing, seek further investigation of all receptacles and make all repairs.
GFCI (Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter) is an automatic device that offers personal protection against lethal electrical shock or electrocution. It is a special electrical receptacle or outlet that can stop electrical power within milliseconds as a safety precaution.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.2 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Switches: Purpose Not Determined
What does this switch operate? One or more switches did not operate. Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.3 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Switches: Installed Improperly
One or more switches are installed improperly. Make repairs as necessary. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

3.2.4 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Fixture: Missing/Damaged Globe
Make repairs as necessary.

3.2.5 II. Electrical Systems: B. Branch Circuits, Connected Devices, and Fixtures - Wiring: Install Dedicated Circuit

4.2.1 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Review HVAC System Sufficiency
Builder/Seller to provide documentation showing the sufficiency of HVAC systems for volume of air conditioned space. 
HVAC professionals can also perform Manual J calculations to determine sufficiency of both heating and cooling systems for volume of air conditioned space. Have the HVAC company recommend upgrades and make all repairs. Data tags of units provided for their use to determine unit capacities.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified heating and cooling contractor

4.2.2 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Water/Debris in Secondary Pan
Remove debris from secondary pan under evaporator coil in attic. Debris may cause premature clogging of condensate line. Clean and service condensate line. Monitor and make repairs as necessary.

4.2.3 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: B. Cooling Equipment - Condensate Drain - Improper Location
Condensate drain line ran to improper location. Recommend qualified technician to run proper drain. Seek further investigation and make necessary repairs.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

4.3.1 III. Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Systems: C. Duct Systems, Chases, and Vents - Vent Damaged
Seek further investigation and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Supply Pressure Too Low
Water supply pressure under 65 PSI, seek licensed plumber to investigate plumbing system and make pressure adjustments (80 max).

5.1.5 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Fixture - No Water Present
Seek further investigation of plumbing system and make all repairs. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

5.1.6 IV. Plumbing Systems: A. Plumbing Supply, Distribution Systems, and Fixtures - Insulate Exterior Plumbing Supply
Exposed water supply lines may freeze in cold weather. Make repairs as necessary.

5.5.1 IV. Plumbing Systems: F. Gas Distribution Systems and Gas Appliances - Rusted Gas Supply
Please be advised all gas piping should be painted in order to prevent rust and corrosion. High quality rust oleum paint is usually the best for metal, steel or galvanized piping. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.1.1 V. Appliances: A. Dishwashers - Improper Installation
Installed crooked/loose under countertop. Make repairs as necessary.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

6.2.1 V. Appliances: B. Food Waste Disposers - Excessive Noise

6.7.1 V. Appliances: G. Garage Door Operators - Door/Gaskets Damaged

6.8.1 V. Appliances: H. Dryer Exhaust Systems - Damaged Dryer Vent

Make repairs as necessary.

3.1.1 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Wiring - Red/Black Wires Present
Breakers with red wires must be connected to their black wire counterparts. This outdated wiring method can lead to electrocution because the circuit may not be completely turned off. Conduct a thorough investigation and make all necessary repairs.
The scenario described indicates a potentially dangerous, out-of-code, or improper installation of a Multi-Wire Branch Circuit (MWBC). In a safe, proper installation, red and black wires (representing two different hot phases) must be tied together at the breaker so they disconnect simultaneously. 
If these wires are on separate single-pole breakers, it is a significant shock hazard because shutting off one breaker does not guarantee the circuit is completely de-energized, leading to potential electrocution, especially when working on split-receptacles or shared neutral devices. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.1 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Corner Pop / Wedge Cracks
Corner popping found at one or more areas. This normally tends to be a non- structural issue when it does occur unless or until either the concrete of the foundation corner starts to deteriorate extensively, or the bottom of the corner brick or stone is exposed excessively or if there is cracking in the above masonry and / or mortar joints above the brick lug of the foundation. It cannot be determined at this point to what extent the wedge crack will progress to, or what affect it will have on other areas around it.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.2 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Soil Erosion

3.1.1 II. Electrical Systems: A. Service Entrance and Panels - Wiring - Red/Black Wires Present
Breakers with red wires must be connected to their black wire counterparts. This outdated wiring method can lead to electrocution because the circuit may not be completely turned off. Conduct a thorough investigation and make all necessary repairs.
The scenario described indicates a potentially dangerous, out-of-code, or improper installation of a Multi-Wire Branch Circuit (MWBC). In a safe, proper installation, red and black wires (representing two different hot phases) must be tied together at the breaker so they disconnect simultaneously. 
If these wires are on separate single-pole breakers, it is a significant shock hazard because shutting off one breaker does not guarantee the circuit is completely de-energized, leading to potential electrocution, especially when working on split-receptacles or shared neutral devices. 
Recommendation: Contact a qualified electrical contractor.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.1 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Corner Pop / Wedge Cracks
Corner popping found at one or more areas. This normally tends to be a non- structural issue when it does occur unless or until either the concrete of the foundation corner starts to deteriorate extensively, or the bottom of the corner brick or stone is exposed excessively or if there is cracking in the above masonry and / or mortar joints above the brick lug of the foundation. It cannot be determined at this point to what extent the wedge crack will progress to, or what affect it will have on other areas around it.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.1.1 I. Structural Systems: A. Foundations - Corner Pop / Wedge Cracks
Corner popping found at one or more areas. This normally tends to be a non- structural issue when it does occur unless or until either the concrete of the foundation corner starts to deteriorate extensively, or the bottom of the corner brick or stone is exposed excessively or if there is cracking in the above masonry and / or mortar joints above the brick lug of the foundation. It cannot be determined at this point to what extent the wedge crack will progress to, or what affect it will have on other areas around it.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.1 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Grading - Improper Drainage
Proper drainage away from the foundation may be needed at various areas. Drainage/slope away from the foundation at all grading areas should be maintained at a minimum 5% slope for 10 feet (6”). 2% slope needed at first 10’ from foundation of hard surfaces (Drives, walks, etc). Balanced moisture maintenance around all perimeter areas of a slab foundation is recommended to prevent unbalanced moisture and/ or excessive dry out that will contribute to unneeded foundation settlement, movement that may affect the entire structure. Water standing or running alongside a foundation after rains or from watering (sprinklers) can cause differential settlement of a foundation. If soil grading is such that water runs alongside a foundation during rains, the water will run under the edge of the foundation and soften or carry away soil that is supporting the foundation.
Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.

2.2.2 I. Structural Systems: B. Grading and Drainage - Gutter - Diverters/Splash blocks

Recommendation: Contact a qualified professional.
